name: USS Fleming (DE 32)
events: 28 Sep 1944 USS Tambor (T/Cdr. W.J. Germershausen, Jr., USN) and USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with USS Le Hardy (Lt.Cdr. E.L. Holtz, USNR), USS Tisdale (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Hatfield, USNR), USS Connolly (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Collier, USNR) and USS Widgeon (Lt. C.W. Engler, USN).
USS Tambor remained out for night exercises with USS Le Hardy and USS Fleming (Lt. K.F. Burgess, Jr., USNR).

29 Sep 1944 USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) and USS Atule (T/Cdr. J.H. Maurer, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with USS Charles R. Greer (Cdr. A. Jackson, Jr., USNR) and USS Fleming (Lt. K.F. Burgess, Jr., USNR).
Later USS Haddock (T/Cdr. J.P. Roach, USN) also joined for night exercises.

3 Oct 1944 USS Tuna (T/Cdr. E.F. Steffanides, Jr., USN), USS Pogy (T/Lt.Cdr. P.G. Molteni, Jr., USN), USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with USS Allen (Lt. J.A. Rowe, USNR), USS Le Hardy (Lt.Cdr. E.L. Holtz, USNR), USS Fleming (Lt. K.F. Burgess, Jr., USNR), USS Connolly (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Collier, USNR) and USS Widgeon (Lt. C.W. Engler, USN).

12 Mar 1945 The escort carrier (in an aircraft ferry role) HMS Ranee (A/Capt. J.A.W. Tothill, DSC, RN) arrived at Guam from Eniwetok. Her escort, the destroyer escort USS Fleming (Lt.Cdr. K.F. Burgess, Jr., USNR) did not enter the harbour but proceeded to Ulithi where she arrived the following day.
At Guam HMS Ranee disembarked the 63 aircraft that had been onboard. This was completed on the 13th. (1)
